description: Sends the tempo duration as modulation signal

parameterGroups.label: Tempo Selection
parameterGroups.rationale: Tempo and Multiplier together define the musical duration to output.

parameterGroups.label: Sync Toggle
parameterGroups.rationale: When disabled, uses a manual time value instead of tempo-synced duration.

parameterGroups.label: Manual Time
parameterGroups.rationale: Used when Enabled=false (sync disabled) to set a manual time in milliseconds.

gaps.question: From tempo.md: output = TempoSyncer::getTempoInMilliSeconds(bpm, currentTempo) * multiplier when Enabled=true, or UnsyncedTime when Enabled=false. Confirm units are always milliseconds.
gaps.context: Since output is unnormalised, the units are critical for users connecting to delay time parameters.

gaps.question: tempo_sync has IsProcessingHiseEvent=true. What events does it respond to? From tempo.md it seems tempo changes come via TempoListener callbacks, not MIDI events. Why is IsProcessingHiseEvent set?
gaps.context: Need to clarify whether MIDI events trigger any behaviour, or if IsProcessingHiseEvent is set for another reason.

gaps.question: From tempo.md: the node only sends an update when the value actually changes (lastMs != currentTempoMilliseconds). Does it also send on voice start?
gaps.context: Users need to know when the output updates to understand timing behaviour.

notes: tempo_sync does NOT have IsControlNode in cppProperties. It uses wrap::mod pattern. It IS a TempoListener (inherits from clock_base). Has IsProcessingHiseEvent. Output is unnormalised (raw milliseconds). The Enabled parameter switches between tempo-synced and manual (UnsyncedTime) modes. Common use case: driving delay time parameters.
